mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for equity valuations, directly depressing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns are not significantly lower during rate hikes.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that trigger recession fears, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the following 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in >55% of triggered instances, or the signal loses predictive power during expansion phases.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer demand strength, which drives corporate earnings growth and produces positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict positive 60-day returns with a statistically significant coefficient (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: An increase in the index of industrial production reflects strengthening manufacturing and utility output, which generates positive short-term market momentum over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases coincide with negative 5-day forward returns in >60% of observations during tightening cycles.

mechanism_narrative: Year-over-year consumer price inflation exceeding 3.0% forces markets to price in aggressive central bank tightening, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL above 3.0% is followed by positive 20-day returns in >50% of cases, or the threshold effect vanishes in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a stable or declining unemployment rate confirms robust economic expansion, supporting equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal yields a negative rank correlation with 60-day forward returns over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real gross domestic product growth directly increases corporate profit expectations, creating a persistent positive relationship with S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth shows no statistically significant predictive power for 20-day forward returns (t-stat < 1.5) across multiple rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in industrial production exceeding 0.5% signals a sharp contraction in economic activity, triggering immediate risk-off selling and negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO drops > 0.5% are followed by positive 5-day returns in >55% of instances, or the signal fails to separate bull and bear market regimes.
